technique: Keep your chin tucked behind your lead shoulder at all times.
Rotate your hips fully on the cross for maximum power.
Return your jab to guard position as fast as you throw it.
Stay on the balls of your feet -- flat-footed fighters get caught.
Your hook should travel in a tight arc, not a wide loop.
Exhale sharply with every punch. Breathing powers the shot.
Keep your elbows tight to your body when not punching.
Sit down on your punches -- power comes from the legs, not the arms.
Turn your fist over at the end of the jab for a clean snap.
When throwing the cross, push off your back foot and pivot.
Uppercuts start from the knees. Bend slightly then drive upward.
Do not telegraph your punches by pulling your hand back first.
Keep your non-punching hand glued to your cheek for protection.
After every combination, reset your feet to a balanced stance.
Relax your shoulders between punches. Tension kills speed.
encouragement: Great work! Your combinations are getting sharper.
Nice speed on that last combo. Keep it up!
You are improving round by round. Stay focused.
That was a clean sequence. Your timing is on point.
Excellent power transfer on those crosses.
Your reaction time is getting better. Keep pushing.
Strong finish to that round. You have real grit.
That combination flowed beautifully. Ring-ready stuff.
You are throwing with purpose now. Every shot counts.
Impressive stamina. You are maintaining quality even when tired.
Your defence is tightening up. Harder to hit every round.
Way to stay disciplined with your guard. Keep it high.
That round showed real improvement. The work is paying off.
correction: Your guard is dropping after the cross. Bring it back to your chin.
You are leaning forward too much. Stay centred over your hips.
Your jab is looping -- keep it straight down the pipe.
You are dropping your right hand when you jab. That is a free shot for your opponent.
Your hooks are too wide. Tighten the arc for more speed and power.
You are standing too square. Angle your stance for a smaller target.
Do not reach for punches. Step into range first, then throw.
Your feet are too close together. Widen your base for stability.
You are holding your breath. Remember to exhale with each punch.
Your uppercuts are coming from too far away. Get close first.
Slow down if your form breaks. Speed without technique is wasted energy.
You are crossing your feet when you move. Slide, do not step over.
Your head stays on the centre line. Move it after you punch.
suggestion: Try doubling up your jab before the cross. It disrupts timing.
Mix in some body shots to open up the head.
Try feinting the jab and going straight to the hook.
Work on moving your head after every combination.
Practice the 1-2-3 until it feels automatic, then add the 4.
Focus on one combination per round until it is second nature.
Try throwing from the southpaw stance for a few rounds.
Add a step to the left after your cross to create an angle.
Work the jab for thirty seconds straight. Volume beats power sometimes.
After blocking, immediately counter with a cross. Train the reflex.
Practice slipping left then throwing the right uppercut.
Try ending each combo with a jab. It resets your position and keeps you safe.
Set a personal speed record on the 1-2 combo this round.
